firstTime.title: Your free WordPress
firstTime.subtitle: Setting things up… have a read while you wait.
firstTime.intro: This is a complete WordPress that runs in your browser: no sign-up, no hosting plan, no domain needed. It's not meant for public blogging. Instead, it's your own personal WordPress, where you can install apps that keep your stuff with you: reading lists, saved articles, contacts, conversations, and more. Your data lives here, and you can shape this WordPress however you like.
bullets: Running WordPress in the browser is unusual. It normally lives on a server. We've tried to make it behave just like a normally hosted WordPress, but you might run into the occasional oddity.

Look for the small Site Tools icon in the bottom-left corner. It's always there, floating above WordPress, and it opens everything you need: install apps, manage files, view logs, and restore backups.

Your WordPress downloads a backup every day, automatically (configurable in Site Tools). If your browser data ever gets cleared, this page will come back as a fresh WordPress. Open Site Tools and point it at your saved backup to restore your old site.
firstTime.bookmarkTip: ⭐ Bookmark this page. This is your WordPress now. Add it to your bookmarks so you can easily come back.

tips: The Site Tools icon in the bottom-left corner is always there. Click it to open Site Tools any time.
Your site backs up automatically every day. Change the schedule or turn it off in Site Tools.

changelog.title: Just WordPress
changelog.text: Before this change, we showed a second address bar. This has now been removed. Like on a hosted WordPress, your browser will show you where you are.

changelog.title: Accessing the Site Tools
changelog.text: The Playground sidebar is now accessible via the Site Tools icon in the bottom-left corner. Click it any time for files, database, logs, backups, and recovery.

changelog.title: Apps launcher
changelog.text: The apps launcher is now installed by default. You can install apps directly from there.
